packages.name: gltf
packages.synopsis: Package gltf is a dependency-free toolkit for reading, writing, and inspecting glTF 2.0 assets in both the JSON (.gltf) and binary (.glb) container formats.
packages.doc:
Package gltf is a dependency-free toolkit for reading, writing, and inspecting
glTF 2.0 assets in both the JSON (.gltf) and binary (.glb) container formats.

# Document model

[Document] is the root object and mirrors the glTF 2.0 JSON schema. Every
top-level collection (scenes, nodes, meshes, accessors, buffer views,
buffers, materials, textures, images, samplers, animations, skins, and
cameras) is a slice addressed by integer index from elsewhere in the
document, exactly as in the specification. Unknown or vendor-specific data is
preserved through the Extensions and Extras fields, which are typed as
[encoding/json.RawMessage].

Typed enumerations ([ComponentType], [AccessorType], [PrimitiveMode],
[Filter], [WrapMode], [Interpolation], [CameraType], [AlphaMode], and others)
carry the spec-defined constant values and helper methods.

# Reading and writing

For JSON documents use [Decode] and [Encode], or the file helpers [Open] and
[Save]. For the binary container use [ReadGLB] and [WriteGLB], or the file
helpers [OpenGLB] and [SaveGLB]. [Open] and [OpenGLB] additionally resolve
buffer data.

# Buffers

Buffer payloads come from three sources: an embedded GLB BIN chunk, a base64
"data:" URI, or an external file. [Document.ResolveBuffers] loads all three
into each [Buffer]'s Data field; [EncodeDataURI] performs the reverse for
embedding.

# Accessors

Once buffers are resolved, the Decode* methods on [Document] read typed
vertex data out of accessors, honoring byteOffset, byteStride, the normalized
flag, and sparse substitutions:

  - [Document.DecodeAccessorFloat32] returns flattened float32 components;
  - [Document.DecodeAccessorVec2], [Document.DecodeAccessorVec3], and
    [Document.DecodeAccessorVec4] return typed vectors;
  - [Document.DecodeAccessorUint32] and [Document.DecodeIndices] return
    unsigned integer index data.

The Add* methods provide the write path: [Document.AddAccessorVec3],
[Document.AddAccessorVec2], [Document.AddAccessorVec4],
[Document.AddAccessorFloat32], and [Document.AddIndicesUint32] append typed
data to buffer 0 as a new bufferView and accessor, computing min/max.

# Scene evaluation

The transform helpers work with a column-major [Mat4], a [Quat] rotation, and
[Vec3] vectors. [TRS] composes a transform; [Mat4.Decompose] recovers it;
[Node.LocalMatrix] and [Document.GlobalMatrix] give local and world
transforms. [Document.EvaluateSampler] and [Document.SampleChannel] evaluate
animation channels (STEP, LINEAR, and CUBICSPLINE, with quaternion [Slerp]
for rotations), and [Document.ApplyAnimation] poses nodes in place.
[Document.JointMatrices] computes skinning matrices, and
[Document.MorphedPositions] blends morph targets.

# Extensions

Named Khronos extensions are modeled by typed structs (for example
[MaterialsUnlit], [MaterialsTransmission], [TextureTransform], and
[LightsPunctual]) with parse/encode helpers [GetExtension] and [SetExtension];
unknown extensions round-trip verbatim through [ExtensionMap] and
[MarshalExtensions].

# Images

[Document.DecodeImage] decodes embedded, data-URI, and external PNG/JPEG
images to an [image.Image].

# Validation

[Document.Validate] performs required-field, index-range, and structural
consistency checks (accessor and bufferView bounds, component/type rules,
animation, camera, material, image, and skin constraints), returning a
[ValidationErrors] value that lists every problem with a descriptive path.

# Building

[Triangle] constructs a minimal, valid single-triangle document plus its
binary buffer; [WriteTriangleGLB] and [WriteTriangleGLTF] emit it directly.

consts.doc:
Extension name constants for the Khronos (KHR_) extensions supported by this
package.

Extension name constants for the additional Khronos PBR material extensions
modeled below.
GLB container constants defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.
Version is the glTF specification version this package targets.

vars.doc: IdentityQuat is the rotation quaternion representing no rotation.
TrianglePositions are the three VEC3 vertex positions used by Triangle.

types.name: Accessor
types.signature:
type Accessor struct {
	BufferView    *Index          `json:"bufferView,omitempty"`
	ByteOffset    int             `json:"byteOffset,omitempty"`
	ComponentType ComponentType   `json:"componentType"`
	Normalized    bool            `json:"normalized,omitempty"`
	Count         int             `json:"count"`
	Type          AccessorType    `json:"type"`
	Max           []float64       `json:"max,omitempty"`
	Min           []float64       `json:"min,omitempty"`
	Sparse        *Sparse         `json:"sparse,omitempty"`
	Name          string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Extensions    json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras        json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
Accessor defines how to interpret the typed data stored in a bufferView (or
generated by a sparse substitution).

types.name: AccessorType
types.signature: type AccessorType string
types.doc:
AccessorType specifies whether an accessor element is a scalar, vector, or
matrix. It is encoded as a JSON string in glTF.

consts.signature:
const (
	AccessorScalar AccessorType = "SCALAR"
	AccessorVec2   AccessorType = "VEC2"
	AccessorVec3   AccessorType = "VEC3"
	AccessorVec4   AccessorType = "VEC4"
	AccessorMat2   AccessorType = "MAT2"
	AccessorMat3   AccessorType = "MAT3"
	AccessorMat4   AccessorType = "MAT4"
)
consts.doc: Accessor element types defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

methods.name: ComponentCount
methods.doc:
ComponentCount returns the number of components in a single element of this
accessor type. It returns 0 for an unknown type.

types.name: AlphaMode
types.signature: type AlphaMode string
types.doc: AlphaMode controls how a material's alpha value is interpreted.

consts.doc: Material alpha modes defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

types.name: Animation
types.signature:
type Animation struct {
	Name       string             `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Channels   []AnimationChannel `json:"channels"`
	Samplers   []AnimationSampler `json:"samplers"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage    `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage    `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: Animation binds keyframe data (samplers) to node properties (channels).

types.name: AnimationChannel
types.doc: AnimationChannel connects an animation sampler to a target property.

types.name: AnimationChannelTarget
types.doc: AnimationChannelTarget identifies the node and property an animation drives.

types.name: AnimationPath
types.signature: type AnimationPath string
types.doc: AnimationPath is the node property targeted by an animation channel.

consts.doc: Animation target paths defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

types.name: AnimationSampler
types.signature:
type AnimationSampler struct {
	Input         Index           `json:"input"`
	Interpolation Interpolation   `json:"interpolation,omitempty"`
	Output        Index           `json:"output"`
	Extensions    json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras        json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: AnimationSampler holds keyframe input (times) and output (values) accessors.

methods.name: GetInterpolation
methods.doc:
GetInterpolation returns the sampler's interpolation algorithm, defaulting to
[InterpolationLinear] when unset, as required by the specification.

types.name: Asset
types.signature:
type Asset struct {
	Copyright  string          `json:"copyright,omitempty"`
	Generator  string          `json:"generator,omitempty"`
	Version    string          `json:"version"`
	MinVersion string          `json:"minVersion,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
Asset holds metadata about the glTF asset. The Version field is required by
the specification.

types.name: Box
types.doc:
Box is an axis-aligned bounding box in world or local space, defined by its
minimum and maximum corner. An empty box (one that contains no points) has a
Min component greater than the corresponding Max component; see [EmptyBox].

funcs.name: EmptyBox
funcs.doc:
EmptyBox returns an empty bounding box: its Min is +Inf and its Max is -Inf on
every axis, so the first point added by [Box.Add] establishes both corners.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the smallest box that contains both b and the point p.

methods.name: Center
methods.doc:
Center returns the midpoint of the box. It is only meaningful for a non-empty
box.

methods.name: Contains
methods.doc:
Contains reports whether the point p lies within the box, inclusive of its
boundary.

methods.name: Empty
methods.doc:
Empty reports whether the box contains no points, which is true when any axis
has Min greater than Max.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc:
Size returns the extent of the box along each axis (Max minus Min). It is only
meaningful for a non-empty box.

methods.name: Transform
methods.doc:
Transform returns the axis-aligned bounding box that encloses b after each of
its eight corners is transformed by the column-major matrix m. An empty box is
returned unchanged.

methods.name: Union
methods.doc:
Union returns the smallest box that contains both b and o. Empty operands are
ignored, so unioning with an [EmptyBox] returns the other box.

types.name: Buffer
types.doc:
Buffer points to binary geometry, animation, or skin data. URI may be a file
path, a base64 data URI, or empty for the GLB binary chunk.

types.name: BufferView
types.signature:
type BufferView struct {
	Buffer     Index           `json:"buffer"`
	ByteOffset int             `json:"byteOffset,omitempty"`
	ByteLength int             `json:"byteLength"`
	ByteStride int             `json:"byteStride,omitempty"`
	Target     *TargetType     `json:"target,omitempty"`
	Name       string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: BufferView is a contiguous slice of a buffer.

types.name: Camera
types.signature:
type Camera struct {
	Name         string              `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Type         CameraType          `json:"type"`
	Perspective  *CameraPerspective  `json:"perspective,omitempty"`
	Orthographic *CameraOrthographic `json:"orthographic,omitempty"`
	Extensions   json.RawMessage     `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras       json.RawMessage     `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
Camera is a projection used to render the scene. Exactly one of Perspective
or Orthographic must be set, matching Type.

methods.name: ProjectionMatrix
methods.doc:
ProjectionMatrix returns the column-major projection matrix for the camera,
dispatching on its Type to the perspective or orthographic projection. It
returns an error when the camera is missing the projection block that matches
its Type.

types.name: CameraOrthographic
types.signature:
type CameraOrthographic struct {
	XMag       float64         `json:"xmag"`
	YMag       float64         `json:"ymag"`
	ZFar       float64         `json:"zfar"`
	ZNear      float64         `json:"znear"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: CameraOrthographic holds orthographic projection parameters.

methods.name: ProjectionMatrix
methods.doc:
ProjectionMatrix returns the column-major projection matrix for the
orthographic camera, following the glTF 2.0 specification.

types.name: CameraPerspective
types.signature:
type CameraPerspective struct {
	AspectRatio *float64        `json:"aspectRatio,omitempty"`
	YFOV        float64         `json:"yfov"`
	ZFar        *float64        `json:"zfar,omitempty"`
	ZNear       float64         `json:"znear"`
	Extensions  json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras      json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: CameraPerspective holds perspective projection parameters.

methods.name: ProjectionMatrix
methods.doc:
ProjectionMatrix returns the column-major projection matrix for the
perspective camera, following the glTF 2.0 specification. When ZFar is nil the
projection uses an infinite far plane. When AspectRatio is nil an aspect ratio
of 1 is assumed (the specification defers to the viewport aspect ratio).

types.name: CameraType
types.signature: type CameraType string
types.doc: CameraType distinguishes perspective and orthographic cameras.

consts.doc: Camera types defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

types.name: ComponentType
types.signature: type ComponentType int
types.doc:
ComponentType identifies the datatype of the components that make up an
accessor element. The integer values match the glTF 2.0 specification (they
are OpenGL constants).

consts.signature:
const (
	ComponentByte          ComponentType = 5120 // signed 8-bit integer
	ComponentUnsignedByte  ComponentType = 5121 // unsigned 8-bit integer
	ComponentShort         ComponentType = 5122 // signed 16-bit integer
	ComponentUnsignedShort ComponentType = 5123 // unsigned 16-bit integer
	ComponentUnsignedInt   ComponentType = 5125 // unsigned 32-bit integer
	ComponentFloat         ComponentType = 5126 // 32-bit IEEE-754 float
)
consts.doc: Component types defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

methods.name: SizeInBytes
methods.doc:
SizeInBytes returns the size, in bytes, of a single component of this type.
It returns 0 for an unknown component type.

methods.name: String
methods.doc: String returns a human-readable name for the component type.

types.name: Document
types.signature:
type Document struct {
	// ExtensionsUsed lists glTF extensions used somewhere in this asset.
	ExtensionsUsed []string `json:"extensionsUsed,omitempty"`
	// ExtensionsRequired lists glTF extensions required to load this asset.
	ExtensionsRequired []string `json:"extensionsRequired,omitempty"`

	Asset       Asset        `json:"asset"`
	Scene       *Index       `json:"scene,omitempty"`
	Scenes      []Scene      `json:"scenes,omitempty"`
	Nodes       []Node       `json:"nodes,omitempty"`
	Meshes      []Mesh       `json:"meshes,omitempty"`
	Accessors   []Accessor   `json:"accessors,omitempty"`
	BufferViews []BufferView `json:"bufferViews,omitempty"`
	Buffers     []Buffer     `json:"buffers,omitempty"`
	Materials   []Material   `json:"materials,omitempty"`
	Textures    []Texture    `json:"textures,omitempty"`
	Images      []Image      `json:"images,omitempty"`
	Samplers    []Sampler    `json:"samplers,omitempty"`
	Animations  []Animation  `json:"animations,omitempty"`
	Skins       []Skin       `json:"skins,omitempty"`
	Cameras     []Camera     `json:"cameras,omitempty"`

	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
Document is the root object of a glTF 2.0 asset. It mirrors the JSON schema
of the glTF specification; every top-level array is addressed by integer
index from elsewhere in the document.

funcs.name: Decode
funcs.doc:
Decode reads a .gltf JSON document from r and returns the parsed Document.
It does not resolve buffer data; call ResolveBuffers for that.

funcs.name: Open
funcs.doc:
Open reads and decodes a .gltf file at path and resolves its buffers relative
to the file's directory. External file and data-URI buffers are loaded.

funcs.name: OpenGLB
funcs.doc:
OpenGLB reads and decodes a .glb file at path, attaching its BIN chunk to the
document's buffers and resolving external and data-URI buffers relative to
the file's directory.

funcs.name: ReadGLB
funcs.doc:
ReadGLB reads a binary GLB stream from r, returning the parsed Document and
the raw bytes of its embedded BIN chunk (nil if absent). The BIN chunk is
also attached to the first buffer without a URI via ResolveBuffers-style
wiring: callers that need buffer data resolved should pass the returned bin
to Document.ResolveBuffers.

funcs.name: Triangle
funcs.doc:
Triangle builds a minimal, valid glTF document describing a single triangle:
one buffer, one bufferView, one POSITION accessor, one primitive, one mesh,
one node, and one scene. It returns the document together with the binary
buffer data (the GLB BIN chunk). Buffer 0 is URI-less and references that
data, which is also attached to the buffer's Data field so the document can
be decoded immediately without calling ResolveBuffers.

methods.name: AccessorBounds
methods.doc:
AccessorBounds returns the axis-aligned bounding box of a VEC3 accessor (for
example a POSITION attribute). When the accessor carries a three-component Min
and Max they are used directly; otherwise the accessor is decoded and scanned.
It returns an error if the accessor is not VEC3 or cannot be decoded.

methods.name: AddAccessorFloat32
methods.doc:
AddAccessorFloat32 appends flattened float32 data as a new accessor of the
given element type to buffer 0, computing per-component min/max, and returns
the new accessor index. len(data) must be a multiple of the type's component
count. The bytes are stored little-endian and, when the document has no
resolved buffer 0, one is created so the data can be decoded immediately.

methods.name: AddAccessorVec2
methods.doc:
AddAccessorVec2 appends VEC2 float data as a new accessor and returns its
index. See [Document.AddAccessorFloat32].

methods.name: AddAccessorVec3
methods.doc:
AddAccessorVec3 appends VEC3 float data (for example positions or normals) as
a new accessor and returns its index. See [Document.AddAccessorFloat32].

methods.name: AddAccessorVec4
methods.doc:
AddAccessorVec4 appends VEC4 float data as a new accessor and returns its
index. See [Document.AddAccessorFloat32].

methods.name: AddBinData
methods.doc:
AddBinData appends raw bytes (for example an encoded PNG or JPEG image) to
buffer 0 and adds a bufferView covering them, returning the new bufferView
index. It is the write-path counterpart to a bufferView-backed [Image].

methods.name: AddIndicesUint32
methods.doc:
AddIndicesUint32 appends unsigned 32-bit index data as a new SCALAR accessor
bound to an element-array bufferView, and returns the new accessor index. It
is the write-path inverse of [Document.DecodeIndices].

methods.name: ApplyAnimation
methods.doc:
ApplyAnimation samples every channel of anim at time t and writes the results
into the targeted nodes' Translation, Rotation, Scale, and Weights fields.
Channels whose target node is nil are skipped. Buffers must be resolved
first. It is a convenience for posing a document at a point in time.

methods.name: DecodeAccessorFloat32
methods.doc:
DecodeAccessorFloat32 decodes accessor at index into a flat slice of
float32 values with length Count*ComponentCount, applying normalization when
the accessor's Normalized flag is set. It works for any component type.

methods.name: DecodeAccessorMat4
methods.doc:
DecodeAccessorMat4 decodes a MAT4 accessor into a slice of column-major
[Mat4] matrices. It is the correct decoder for a skin's inverse bind
matrices.

methods.name: DecodeAccessorUint32
methods.doc:
DecodeAccessorUint32 decodes an accessor with an unsigned-integer component
type into a flat slice of uint32 values (length Count*ComponentCount). It is
the correct decoder for index accessors (SCALAR UNSIGNED_BYTE/SHORT/INT).

methods.name: DecodeAccessorVec2
methods.doc:
DecodeAccessorVec2 decodes a VEC2 accessor into a slice of 2-component
float32 vectors.

methods.name: DecodeAccessorVec3
methods.doc:
DecodeAccessorVec3 decodes a VEC3 accessor into a slice of 3-component
float32 vectors (for example vertex positions or normals).

methods.name: DecodeAccessorVec4
methods.doc:
DecodeAccessorVec4 decodes a VEC4 accessor into a slice of 4-component
float32 vectors.

methods.name: DecodeImage
methods.signature: func (d *Document) DecodeImage(imageIndex int, baseDir string) (image.Image, string, error)
methods.doc:
DecodeImage decodes the image at imageIndex into an [image.Image], returning
the registered format name (for example "png" or "jpeg"). PNG and JPEG are
supported out of the box; register additional decoders via the standard
library's image package to handle more formats. baseDir resolves external
file URIs and may be empty for embedded images.

methods.name: DecodeIndices
methods.doc:
DecodeIndices decodes the primitive's index accessor into a flat slice of
uint32 vertex indices. It returns nil, nil for a non-indexed primitive.

methods.name: DecodeMorphTargetVec3
methods.signature: func (d *Document) DecodeMorphTargetVec3(prim *Primitive, targetIndex int, attribute string) ([][3]float32, error)
methods.doc:
DecodeMorphTargetVec3 decodes the VEC3 delta values of a single morph target
attribute (for example "POSITION" or "NORMAL") on a primitive. targetIndex
selects the entry in the primitive's Targets slice. Buffers must be resolved
first.

methods.name: DecodeMorphTargetsVec3
methods.doc:
DecodeMorphTargetsVec3 decodes the VEC3 deltas of the given attribute for
every morph target of the primitive, returning one delta slice per target.
Buffers must be resolved first.

methods.name: EvaluateSampler
methods.signature: func (d *Document) EvaluateSampler(s *AnimationSampler, t float64, rotation bool) ([]float32, error)
methods.doc:
EvaluateSampler evaluates the animation sampler at time t, returning the
interpolated value as a slice of stride float32 components. Times outside the
keyframe range are clamped to the first or last keyframe. When rotation is
true (a rotation channel), LINEAR interpolation uses quaternion slerp and the
CUBICSPLINE result is normalized, matching the glTF specification. Buffers
must be resolved first.

methods.name: GlobalMatrices
methods.doc:
GlobalMatrices returns the world (global) transform of every node in the
document, indexed by node index. Each matrix is the product of the local
matrices from the node's root ancestor down to the node. It returns an error
if the node hierarchy contains a cycle.

methods.name: GlobalMatrix
methods.doc:
GlobalMatrix returns the world (global) transform of the node at nodeIndex,
obtained by multiplying the local matrices of every ancestor from the root
down to the node. It returns an error if nodeIndex is out of range or the
hierarchy contains a cycle.

methods.name: ImageBytes
methods.signature: func (d *Document) ImageBytes(imageIndex int, baseDir string) ([]byte, string, error)

methods.name: InverseBindMatrices
methods.doc:
InverseBindMatrices returns the skin's inverse bind matrices. When the skin
declares an inverseBindMatrices accessor it is decoded; otherwise the glTF
default of one identity matrix per joint is returned. Buffers must be
resolved first when an accessor is present.

methods.name: JointMatrices
methods.doc:
JointMatrices computes the skinning matrix for each joint of the skin as
globalJointTransform * inverseBindMatrix. The result has one matrix per
joint, in joint order. This is the joint matrix used by vertex skinning when
the skinned mesh node's own transform is identity; use [Document.JointMatricesForNode]
to account for a non-identity mesh node. Buffers must be resolved first.

methods.name: JointMatricesForNode
methods.doc:
JointMatricesForNode computes joint matrices for the skin as it is used by
the mesh node meshNodeIndex, following the glTF formula

	jointMatrix(j) = inverse(globalTransform(meshNode)) *
	                 globalTransform(joints[j]) * inverseBindMatrices[j]

The mesh node's inverse global transform maps joints into the mesh's local
space. Buffers must be resolved first.

methods.name: Lights
methods.doc:
Lights decodes the document-level KHR_lights_punctual extension, returning
its light array and whether the extension is present.

methods.name: MorphedPositions
methods.doc:
MorphedPositions returns a primitive's POSITION attribute with the primitive's
morph targets applied at the given weights. It is a convenience combining
[Document.DecodeAccessorVec3], [Document.DecodeMorphTargetsVec3], and
[BlendMorphTargetsVec3]. Buffers must be resolved first.

methods.name: NodesInScene
methods.doc:
NodesInScene returns the indices of every node reachable from the scene at
sceneIndex, obtained by a depth-first walk of the scene's root nodes and their
descendants. Each node appears once, in depth-first pre-order. It returns an
error for an out-of-range scene index or a cycle in the node hierarchy.

methods.name: PrimitiveBounds
methods.doc:
PrimitiveBounds returns the local-space bounding box of a primitive, computed
from its POSITION attribute. It returns an error when the primitive has no
POSITION attribute or its accessor cannot be read.

methods.name: ResolveBuffers
methods.doc:
ResolveBuffers loads the raw bytes for every buffer in the document, storing
them in each Buffer's Data field. It handles three sources:

  - an embedded GLB BIN chunk (a buffer with an empty URI), supplied via bin;
  - base64 "data:" URIs;
  - external files, resolved relative to baseDir.

baseDir may be empty when no external file references are expected. bin may
be nil when there is no GLB binary chunk.

methods.name: RootNodes
methods.doc:
RootNodes returns the indices of every node that is not referenced as a child
of another node, in ascending order. These are the roots of the document's
node forest, regardless of which scene they belong to.

methods.name: SampleChannel
methods.signature: func (d *Document) SampleChannel(anim *Animation, channelIndex int, t float64) (AnimationPath, []float32, error)
methods.doc:
SampleChannel evaluates animation channel channelIndex of anim at time t. It
returns the target property path and the interpolated value: three components
for translation/scale, four (a quaternion) for rotation, and one per morph
target for weights. Buffers must be resolved first.

methods.name: SamplerKeyframes
methods.signature: func (d *Document) SamplerKeyframes(s *AnimationSampler) (times []float32, values []float32, stride int, err error)
methods.doc:
SamplerKeyframes decodes an animation sampler's input (times) and output
(values) accessors. times has one entry per keyframe. values is flattened
with stride perKeyframe components; for CUBICSPLINE each keyframe occupies
three consecutive strides (in-tangent, value, out-tangent). Buffers must be
resolved first.

methods.name: SceneBounds
methods.doc:
SceneBounds returns the world-space bounding box of every mesh reachable from
the scene at sceneIndex, transforming each primitive's local bounds by the
global matrix of the node that carries it. An empty scene (no meshes) yields
an empty box. It returns an error for an out-of-range scene index or a node
hierarchy cycle.

methods.name: Validate
methods.doc:
Validate checks the document for required fields and index ranges, returning
a ValidationErrors describing every problem found, or nil if the document is
structurally valid. It does not require buffer data to be resolved.

types.name: Filter
types.signature: type Filter int
types.doc: Filter identifies a texture magnification or minification filter.

consts.signature:
const (
	FilterNearest              Filter = 9728
	FilterLinear               Filter = 9729
	FilterNearestMipmapNearest Filter = 9984
	FilterLinearMipmapNearest  Filter = 9985
	FilterNearestMipmapLinear  Filter = 9986
	FilterLinearMipmapLinear   Filter = 9987
)
consts.doc: Texture filters defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String returns the glTF specification name of the texture filter (for example
"LINEAR_MIPMAP_LINEAR"), or a numeric fallback for an unknown value.

types.name: Image
types.signature:
type Image struct {
	Name       string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	URI        string          `json:"uri,omitempty"`
	MimeType   string          `json:"mimeType,omitempty"`
	BufferView *Index          `json:"bufferView,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
Image is image data used to create a texture. Exactly one of URI or
BufferView is expected.

types.name: Index
types.signature: type Index = int
types.doc:
Index is a reference to another element in one of the Document's top-level
arrays. glTF omits absent indices from the JSON, so it is represented as a
pointer to an int; a nil pointer means "no reference".

types.name: Interpolation
types.signature: type Interpolation string
types.doc: Interpolation is the interpolation algorithm used by an animation sampler.

consts.doc: Animation interpolation algorithms defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

types.name: Light
types.doc: Light is a punctual light source defined by KHR_lights_punctual.

types.name: LightSpot
types.doc: LightSpot holds the cone angles of a spot light.

types.name: LightType
types.signature: type LightType string
types.doc: LightType is the kind of a punctual light.

consts.doc: Punctual light types defined by KHR_lights_punctual.

types.name: LightsPunctual
types.doc:
LightsPunctual is the KHR_lights_punctual document-level extension, holding
the array of lights that nodes reference by index.

types.name: Mat4
types.signature: type Mat4 [16]float64
types.doc:
Mat4 is a 4x4 matrix stored in column-major order, matching the layout of a
glTF [Node] matrix: element (row r, column c) is at index c*4+r.

funcs.name: IdentityMatrix
funcs.doc: IdentityMatrix returns the 4x4 identity matrix.

funcs.name: LookAt
funcs.signature: func LookAt(eye, center, up Vec3) Mat4
funcs.doc:
LookAt returns a right-handed view matrix that positions the camera at eye
looking toward center, with up giving the approximate world up direction. It
matches the convention of gluLookAt: the resulting matrix maps world space
into the camera's view space (camera looking down its local -Z axis).

funcs.name: Orthographic
funcs.signature: func Orthographic(xmag, ymag, near, far float64) Mat4
funcs.doc:
Orthographic returns a right-handed orthographic projection matrix using the
glTF camera convention. xmag and ymag are the horizontal and vertical
magnifications (half-extents), and near/far are the clip-plane distances.

funcs.name: Perspective
funcs.signature: func Perspective(yfov, aspect, near, far float64) Mat4
funcs.doc:
Perspective returns a right-handed perspective projection matrix using the
glTF camera convention. yfov is the vertical field of view in radians, aspect
is width/height, and near/far are the clip-plane distances. A non-positive
far value produces an infinite-far projection.

funcs.name: TRS
funcs.signature: func TRS(t Vec3, r Quat, s Vec3) Mat4
funcs.doc:
TRS composes a translation, rotation, and scale into a single column-major
transform matrix, equivalent to T * R * S: scale is applied first, then
rotation, then translation.

methods.name: At
methods.signature: func (m Mat4) At(row, col int) float64
methods.doc: At returns the element at the given row and column (both 0-based).

methods.name: Decompose
methods.signature: func (m Mat4) Decompose() (translation Vec3, rotation Quat, scale Vec3)
methods.doc:
Decompose extracts the translation, rotation, and scale that produce the
matrix when combined by [TRS]. It assumes m is a valid affine transform
(no shear); a negative determinant is folded into the X scale.

methods.name: Inverse
methods.doc:
Inverse returns the inverse of the matrix and reports whether it is
invertible. When the matrix is singular the identity matrix is returned with
ok=false.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the matrix product m*n, using column-major conventions. When m
and n are transforms, the result applies n first and then m.

methods.name: MulVec4
methods.doc: MulVec4 returns the product of the matrix and the column vector v.

methods.name: TransformDir
methods.doc:
TransformDir applies the matrix to a direction vector (implicit w=0), so the
translation component is ignored. It is the correct transform for normals'
tangent directions but not for normals themselves under non-uniform scale.

methods.name: TransformPoint
methods.doc:
TransformPoint applies the matrix to a 3D point (implicit w=1) and performs
the perspective divide when the resulting w is not 1.

methods.name: Transpose
methods.doc: Transpose returns the matrix transpose of m.

types.name: Material
types.signature:
type Material struct {
	Name                 string                `json:"name,omitempty"`
	PBRMetallicRoughness *PBRMetallicRoughness `json:"pbrMetallicRoughness,omitempty"`
	NormalTexture        *NormalTexture        `json:"normalTexture,omitempty"`
	OcclusionTexture     *OcclusionTexture     `json:"occlusionTexture,omitempty"`
	EmissiveTexture      *TextureInfo          `json:"emissiveTexture,omitempty"`
	EmissiveFactor       *[3]float64           `json:"emissiveFactor,omitempty"`
	AlphaMode            AlphaMode             `json:"alphaMode,omitempty"`
	AlphaCutoff          *float64              `json:"alphaCutoff,omitempty"`
	DoubleSided          bool                  `json:"doubleSided,omitempty"`
	Extensions           json.RawMessage       `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras               json.RawMessage       `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: Material defines the appearance of a primitive.

methods.name: Clearcoat
methods.doc:
Clearcoat decodes the KHR_materials_clearcoat extension from the material,
reporting whether it is present.

methods.name: EmissiveStrength
methods.doc:
EmissiveStrength returns the KHR_materials_emissive_strength value and whether
the extension is present. When absent it returns the default 1.0.

methods.name: IOR
methods.doc:
IOR returns the KHR_materials_ior index of refraction and whether the
extension is present. When absent it returns the default 1.5.

methods.name: SetExtension
methods.signature: func (m *Material) SetExtension(name string, v any) error
methods.doc:
SetExtension sets or replaces a named extension on the material, preserving
any other extensions already present.

methods.name: Sheen
methods.doc:
Sheen decodes the KHR_materials_sheen extension from the material, reporting
whether it is present.

methods.name: Specular
methods.doc:
Specular decodes the KHR_materials_specular extension from the material,
reporting whether it is present.

methods.name: SpecularGlossiness
methods.doc:
SpecularGlossiness decodes the KHR_materials_pbrSpecularGlossiness extension,
reporting whether it is present.

methods.name: Transmission
methods.doc:
Transmission decodes the KHR_materials_transmission extension, reporting
whether it is present.

methods.name: Unlit
methods.doc: Unlit reports whether the material carries the KHR_materials_unlit extension.

methods.name: Volume
methods.doc:
Volume decodes the KHR_materials_volume extension from the material, reporting
whether it is present.

types.name: MaterialsClearcoat
types.doc:
MaterialsClearcoat is the KHR_materials_clearcoat extension, adding a clear
protective coating layer (as on car paint or lacquered wood) over the base
material.

types.name: MaterialsEmissiveStrength
types.doc:
MaterialsEmissiveStrength is the KHR_materials_emissive_strength extension,
scaling a material's emissive factor beyond the usual [0,1] range.

types.name: MaterialsIOR
types.doc:
MaterialsIOR is the KHR_materials_ior extension, overriding a material's
index of refraction.

types.name: MaterialsPBRSpecularGlossiness
types.doc:
MaterialsPBRSpecularGlossiness is the (archived) KHR_materials_pbrSpecularGlossiness
extension, providing the specular-glossiness material model as an alternative
to metallic-roughness.

types.name: MaterialsSheen
types.doc:
MaterialsSheen is the KHR_materials_sheen extension, adding a retro-reflective
sheen used to model cloth and fabric.

types.name: MaterialsSpecular
types.doc:
MaterialsSpecular is the KHR_materials_specular extension, allowing the
specular reflection strength and color of a metallic-roughness material to be
tuned independently.

types.name: MaterialsTransmission
types.doc:
MaterialsTransmission is the KHR_materials_transmission extension, describing
optically transparent surfaces such as glass.

types.name: MaterialsUnlit
types.doc:
MaterialsUnlit is the KHR_materials_unlit extension. It carries no data; its
presence marks a material as unlit (rendered with its base color only).

types.name: MaterialsVolume
types.doc:
MaterialsVolume is the KHR_materials_volume extension, giving a transmissive
surface a physical thickness so light is attenuated as it passes through the
enclosed volume.

types.name: Mesh
types.signature:
type Mesh struct {
	Name       string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Primitives []Primitive     `json:"primitives"`
	Weights    []float64       `json:"weights,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: Mesh is a set of primitives to be rendered.

types.name: Node
types.signature:
type Node struct {
	Name        string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Camera      *Index          `json:"camera,omitempty"`
	Children    []Index         `json:"children,omitempty"`
	Skin        *Index          `json:"skin,omitempty"`
	Matrix      *[16]float64    `json:"matrix,omitempty"`
	Mesh        *Index          `json:"mesh,omitempty"`
	Rotation    *[4]float64     `json:"rotation,omitempty"`
	Scale       *[3]float64     `json:"scale,omitempty"`
	Translation *[3]float64     `json:"translation,omitempty"`
	Weights     []float64       `json:"weights,omitempty"`
	Extensions  json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras      json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
Node is a node in the scene graph. A node may have either a Matrix or a
combination of Translation, Rotation, and Scale, but not both.

methods.name: LocalMatrix
methods.doc:
LocalMatrix returns the node's local transform as a column-major matrix. If
the node has an explicit matrix it is returned directly; otherwise the
translation, rotation, and scale (each defaulting to identity) are composed
with [TRS].

methods.name: NodeLight
methods.doc:
NodeLight decodes the node-level KHR_lights_punctual reference, returning the
referenced light index and whether the extension is present.

types.name: NodeLight
types.doc:
NodeLight is the KHR_lights_punctual node-level extension, referencing a
light in the document-level [LightsPunctual] array.

types.name: NormalTexture
types.signature:
type NormalTexture struct {
	Index      Index           `json:"index"`
	TexCoord   int             `json:"texCoord,omitempty"`
	Scale      *float64        `json:"scale,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: NormalTexture references a normal map, with an additional scale factor.

types.name: OcclusionTexture
types.signature:
type OcclusionTexture struct {
	Index      Index           `json:"index"`
	TexCoord   int             `json:"texCoord,omitempty"`
	Strength   *float64        `json:"strength,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: OcclusionTexture references an occlusion map, with an additional strength.

types.name: PBRMetallicRoughness
types.signature:
type PBRMetallicRoughness struct {
	BaseColorFactor          *[4]float64     `json:"baseColorFactor,omitempty"`
	BaseColorTexture         *TextureInfo    `json:"baseColorTexture,omitempty"`
	MetallicFactor           *float64        `json:"metallicFactor,omitempty"`
	RoughnessFactor          *float64        `json:"roughnessFactor,omitempty"`
	MetallicRoughnessTexture *TextureInfo    `json:"metallicRoughnessTexture,omitempty"`
	Extensions               json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras                   json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: PBRMetallicRoughness holds the metallic-roughness material model parameters.

types.name: Primitive
types.signature:
type Primitive struct {
	Attributes map[string]Index   `json:"attributes"`
	Indices    *Index             `json:"indices,omitempty"`
	Material   *Index             `json:"material,omitempty"`
	Mode       *PrimitiveMode     `json:"mode,omitempty"`
	Targets    []map[string]Index `json:"targets,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage    `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage    `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
Primitive describes the geometry to be rendered with a material. Attributes
maps attribute semantics (for example "POSITION" or "NORMAL") to accessor
indices.

methods.name: GetMode
methods.doc:
GetMode returns the primitive's rendering mode, defaulting to
PrimitiveTriangles when unset, as required by the specification.

types.name: PrimitiveMode
types.signature: type PrimitiveMode int
types.doc:
PrimitiveMode specifies the type of primitives to render. The integer values
match the glTF 2.0 specification (OpenGL primitive modes).

consts.signature:
const (
	PrimitivePoints        PrimitiveMode = 0
	PrimitiveLines         PrimitiveMode = 1
	PrimitiveLineLoop      PrimitiveMode = 2
	PrimitiveLineStrip     PrimitiveMode = 3
	PrimitiveTriangles     PrimitiveMode = 4
	PrimitiveTriangleStrip PrimitiveMode = 5
	PrimitiveTriangleFan   PrimitiveMode = 6
)
consts.doc: Primitive rendering modes defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String returns the glTF specification name of the primitive mode (for example
"TRIANGLES"), or a numeric fallback for an unknown value.

types.name: Quat
types.signature: type Quat [4]float64
types.doc:
Quat is a rotation quaternion stored in glTF component order (x, y, z, w).
The identity rotation is [0, 0, 0, 1].

funcs.name: QuatFromAxisAngle
funcs.signature: func QuatFromAxisAngle(axis Vec3, angle float64) Quat
funcs.doc:
QuatFromAxisAngle returns the unit quaternion representing a rotation of angle
radians about axis. A zero-length axis yields the identity rotation.

funcs.name: QuatFromEuler
funcs.signature: func QuatFromEuler(x, y, z float64) Quat
funcs.doc:
QuatFromEuler returns the unit quaternion for the given intrinsic Euler
angles in radians, applied in X, then Y, then Z order (roll, pitch, yaw).
The result equals QuatFromAxisAngle(Z,z) * QuatFromAxisAngle(Y,y) *
QuatFromAxisAngle(X,x).

funcs.name: Slerp
funcs.signature: func Slerp(a, b Quat, t float64) Quat
funcs.doc:
Slerp returns the spherical linear interpolation between quaternions a and b
at parameter t in [0,1]. Both are treated as unit quaternions; the result is
normalized. It is the interpolation glTF uses for rotation channels.

methods.name: Conjugate
methods.doc:
Conjugate returns the quaternion conjugate (-x, -y, -z, w). For a unit
quaternion the conjugate is also its inverse.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the four-component dot product of q and o.

methods.name: Matrix
methods.doc:
Matrix returns the 4x4 rotation matrix equivalent to the quaternion. The
quaternion is normalized first.

methods.name: Mul
methods.doc:
Mul returns the Hamilton product q*o. When both are unit quaternions the
result represents applying rotation o first and then q, and is itself a unit
quaternion. Components are in glTF order (x, y, z, w).

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns the quaternion scaled to unit length. A zero quaternion is
returned as the identity.

methods.name: Rotate
methods.doc:
Rotate applies the rotation represented by q to the vector v and returns the
rotated vector. The quaternion is normalized first.

types.name: Sampler
types.signature:
type Sampler struct {
	Name       string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	MagFilter  *Filter         `json:"magFilter,omitempty"`
	MinFilter  *Filter         `json:"minFilter,omitempty"`
	WrapS      *WrapMode       `json:"wrapS,omitempty"`
	WrapT      *WrapMode       `json:"wrapT,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: Sampler defines texture filtering and wrapping.

types.name: Scene
types.doc: Scene is the root of a node hierarchy.

types.name: Skin
types.signature:
type Skin struct {
	Name                string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	InverseBindMatrices *Index          `json:"inverseBindMatrices,omitempty"`
	Skeleton            *Index          `json:"skeleton,omitempty"`
	Joints              []Index         `json:"joints"`
	Extensions          json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras              json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: Skin defines joints and matrices for vertex skinning.

types.name: Sparse
types.signature:
type Sparse struct {
	Count      int             `json:"count"`
	Indices    SparseIndices   `json:"indices"`
	Values     SparseValues    `json:"values"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: Sparse describes displacements applied to a subset of an accessor's elements.

types.name: SparseIndices
types.signature:
type SparseIndices struct {
	BufferView    Index           `json:"bufferView"`
	ByteOffset    int             `json:"byteOffset,omitempty"`
	ComponentType ComponentType   `json:"componentType"`
	Extensions    json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras        json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
SparseIndices points to the bufferView holding the indices of the elements to
be replaced. ComponentType must be an unsigned integer type.

types.name: SparseValues
types.doc: SparseValues points to the bufferView holding the replacement element values.

types.name: TargetType
types.signature: type TargetType int
types.doc: TargetType is the OpenGL buffer binding target hint carried by a bufferView.

consts.doc: BufferView target hints defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String returns the glTF specification name of the bufferView target (for
example "ARRAY_BUFFER"), or a numeric fallback for an unknown value.

types.name: Texture
types.signature:
type Texture struct {
	Name       string          `json:"name,omitempty"`
	Sampler    *Index          `json:"sampler,omitempty"`
	Source     *Index          `json:"source,omitempty"`
	Extensions json.RawMessage `json:"extensions,omitempty"`
	Extras     json.RawMessage `json:"extras,omitempty"`
}
types.doc: Texture pairs a source image with a sampler.

types.name: TextureInfo
types.doc: TextureInfo references a texture and the UV set used to sample it.

methods.name: TextureTransform
methods.doc:
TextureTransform decodes the KHR_texture_transform extension from a texture
reference, reporting whether it is present.

types.name: TextureTransform
types.doc:
TextureTransform is the KHR_texture_transform extension, applying an offset,
rotation, and scale to a texture's UV coordinates. It appears inside a
[TextureInfo]'s extensions.

methods.name: UVMatrix
methods.doc:
UVMatrix returns the 3x3 UV transform (column-major, with the last column
carrying the translation) implied by the texture transform, applying scale,
then rotation, then offset. Absent fields use their glTF defaults.

types.name: ValidationError
types.doc:
ValidationError describes a single problem found by Validate. Path locates the
offending element within the document (for example "meshes[0].primitives[1]").

methods.name: Error
methods.doc: Error implements the error interface.

types.name: ValidationErrors
types.signature: type ValidationErrors []ValidationError
types.doc:
ValidationErrors is a collection of validation problems. It is returned as a
single error by Validate when the document is invalid.

funcs.name: AsValidationErrors
funcs.doc:
AsValidationErrors returns the underlying ValidationErrors when err was
produced by Validate, and reports whether the conversion succeeded.

methods.name: Error
methods.doc: Error implements the error interface, joining each problem on its own line.

types.name: Vec3
types.signature: type Vec3 [3]float64
types.doc:
Vec3 is a three-component vector of float64 values. It is used for node
translation and scale, and as a general-purpose 3D vector by the transform
helpers.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc: Add returns the component-wise sum v+o.

methods.name: Cross
methods.doc: Cross returns the cross product v×o, a vector perpendicular to both operands.

methods.name: Dot
methods.doc: Dot returns the dot product of v and o.

methods.name: Length
methods.doc: Length returns the Euclidean length (magnitude) of v.

methods.name: Lerp
methods.signature: func (v Vec3) Lerp(o Vec3, t float64) Vec3
methods.doc:
Lerp returns the linear interpolation between v and o at parameter t. t=0
yields v and t=1 yields o; values outside [0,1] extrapolate.

methods.name: Normalize
methods.doc:
Normalize returns v scaled to unit length. A zero-length vector is returned
unchanged (as the zero vector).

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale returns v with every component multiplied by s.

methods.name: Sub
methods.doc: Sub returns the component-wise difference v-o.

types.name: Vec4
types.signature: type Vec4 [4]float64
types.doc: Vec4 is a four-component vector of float64 values.

types.name: WrapMode
types.signature: type WrapMode int
types.doc: WrapMode identifies a texture wrapping mode for the S or T axis.

consts.doc: Texture wrapping modes defined by the glTF 2.0 specification.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String returns the glTF specification name of the wrap mode (for example
"CLAMP_TO_EDGE"), or a numeric fallback for an unknown value.

funcs.name: BlendMorphTargetsVec3
funcs.doc:
BlendMorphTargetsVec3 applies weighted morph-target deltas to base vertex
data, returning base + sum(weights[i] * targets[i]). Each target slice and
base must have the same length; weights shorter than targets treat missing
entries as zero. The base slice is not modified.

funcs.name: EffectiveWeights
funcs.doc:
EffectiveWeights returns the morph-target weights in effect for the given
mesh when instantiated by node. Per the specification, a node's weights
override the mesh's default weights; when neither is present a nil slice is
returned. node may be nil to use only the mesh weights.

funcs.name: Encode
funcs.doc: Encode writes doc as indented .gltf JSON to w.

funcs.name: EncodeDataURI
funcs.doc:
EncodeDataURI returns a base64 application/octet-stream data URI for data,
suitable for use as a Buffer.URI in an embedded .gltf file.

funcs.name: ExtensionMap
funcs.doc:
ExtensionMap decodes a raw extensions object into a map from extension name
to its raw JSON value. It returns an empty (non-nil) map when raw is empty.
The map preserves every extension verbatim, including ones this package does
not model, so it can be round-tripped with [MarshalExtensions].

funcs.name: GetExtension
funcs.signature: func GetExtension(raw json.RawMessage, name string, v any) (bool, error)
funcs.doc:
GetExtension decodes the named extension from raw into v, reporting whether
the extension was present. v must be a non-nil pointer.

funcs.name: MarshalExtensions
funcs.doc:
MarshalExtensions encodes an extension map back into a raw extensions object.
It returns nil (which serializes as an omitted field) when the map is empty.

funcs.name: MarshalJSON
funcs.doc:
MarshalJSON is a convenience wrapper returning the indented JSON encoding of
doc as a byte slice.

funcs.name: Save
funcs.doc:
Save encodes doc and writes it to a .gltf file at path. Buffer data is not
written to separate files; callers embedding buffers should use data URIs or
the GLB container.

funcs.name: SaveGLB
funcs.signature: func SaveGLB(path string, doc *Document, bin []byte) error
funcs.doc: SaveGLB writes doc and an optional BIN chunk to a .glb file at path.

funcs.name: SetExtension
funcs.signature: func SetExtension(raw json.RawMessage, name string, v any) (json.RawMessage, error)
funcs.doc:
SetExtension returns a new raw extensions object with the named extension set
to the JSON encoding of v, preserving every other extension already present
in raw. Passing a nil v removes the named extension.

funcs.name: WriteGLB
funcs.signature: func WriteGLB(w io.Writer, doc *Document, bin []byte) error
funcs.doc:
WriteGLB writes doc and an optional BIN chunk to w as a binary GLB stream. If
bin is nil, the BIN chunk is omitted. The JSON chunk is padded with spaces
and the BIN chunk with zeros to a four-byte boundary, as required.

funcs.name: WriteTriangleGLB
funcs.doc: WriteTriangleGLB writes the single-triangle asset to w as a binary .glb.

funcs.name: WriteTriangleGLTF
funcs.doc:
WriteTriangleGLTF writes the single-triangle asset to w as an embedded .gltf,
with the buffer stored as a base64 data URI so the file is self-contained.

examples.doc:
Example builds a single-triangle mesh, writes it to an in-memory GLB
container, reads it back, resolves the embedded buffer, and prints the number
of decoded vertices.
